📊 FinCalc Lab
Calculators Guides About

Financial answers in seconds

32 free calculators for loans, savings, retirement, taxes and debt — each with a plain-English explanation of the math behind the result. No sign-up. Nothing tracked. Everything runs in your browser.

32 calculators 11 in-depth guides 100% free & private

All calculators

Savings & Investing

📈 Compound Interest Calculator Project how savings grow with monthly deposits and compounding. 🎯 Savings Goal Calculator Find the exact monthly saving needed to hit a target amount. 🌴 Retirement Savings Calculator Project your nest egg and the income it could support. 📊 Investment Return (CAGR) Calculator Turn a total gain into an honest annualized return. 💹 ROI Calculator Total and annualized return on any investment or project. ⏳ Rule of 72 Calculator Estimate doubling time from an interest or growth rate. 🕐 Present Value Calculator Discount a future sum back to today's dollars. 🪙 Dividend Income Calculator Annual dividend income, yield and reinvested growth.

Retirement

🏦 401(k) Calculator Project 401(k) growth including the employer match. 💰 Roth IRA Calculator Project tax-free Roth IRA growth from regular contributions.

Loans & Debt

💳 Loan Payment Calculator Monthly payment, total interest and cost of any amortized loan. 🏠 Mortgage Calculator Full monthly housing cost: loan, property tax and insurance. 🚀 Loan Payoff Calculator See how extra monthly payments shorten a loan and cut interest. 🧊 Debt Avalanche vs Snowball Calculator Compare the two classic payoff strategies on your own debts. 🚗 Car Affordability Calculator Turn a monthly budget into a realistic car price. 💳 Credit Card Payoff Calculator How long to clear a card, and what interest costs you. ⚖️ Debt-to-Income (DTI) Calculator The ratio lenders use to approve mortgages and loans.

Income & Budgeting

💰 Salary to Hourly Calculator Convert annual salary to hourly, weekly and monthly pay. 🧾 Take-Home Pay Estimator Estimate net monthly pay from gross salary and tax rates. 💸 50/30/20 Budget Calculator Split your take-home pay into needs, wants and savings. 🛟 Emergency Fund Calculator Size your emergency fund and see how long it takes to build. 🎈 Inflation Calculator See what today's money will be worth in the future. 📋 Net Worth Calculator Add up what you own, subtract what you owe. 📆 Hourly to Salary Calculator Convert an hourly wage into weekly, monthly and yearly pay. 🏡 Rent Affordability Calculator A sensible rent range based on your income.

Taxes & Shopping

🧾 Sales Tax Calculator Add sales tax to a price, or back it out of a total. 🏷️ Discount Calculator Final price after a percentage off, and what you save. 🍽️ Tip Calculator Work out the tip and split a bill between people. 🧾 VAT Calculator Add VAT to a net price or extract it from a gross price.

Business

⚖️ Break-Even Point Calculator How many sales cover your costs — the first business question. 📉 Profit Margin Calculator Margin, markup and profit from cost and price. 🧑‍💻 Freelance Hourly Rate Calculator Turn a target income into the hourly rate you must charge.

Guides & explainers

"The 50/30/20 Rule in Real Life: Where It Works, Where It Breaks, How to Fix It" An honest field guide to the most famous budgeting rule — including what to do when rent alone eats 45% of your income. "APR vs APY: The Difference That Costs (or Earns) You Money" APR and APY look alike but mean different things. Understand compounding, why lenders quote APR and banks quote APY, and how to compare fairly. How Compound Interest Builds Wealth (and Why Starting Late Costs So Much) A plain-English walkthrough of compound growth, with real numbers showing why time in the market beats timing — and what starting five years late actually costs. "Emergency Fund vs Investing: Which Dollar Goes Where First?" A decision framework for the classic dilemma — cash safety versus market growth — including the order of operations most planners actually recommend. "How Much House Can You Afford? The 28/36 Rule and Its Honest Limits" A practical framework for setting your housing budget — the 28/36 rule, the costs first-time buyers forget, and when the guidelines mislead. "Marginal vs Effective Tax Rate: The Misunderstanding That Costs People Real Money" Why "being in the 24% bracket" doesn't mean losing 24% of your income, how progressive brackets actually work, and the raise myth debunked with numbers. "Why Net Worth Is the Only Number That Really Matters" Income tells you how fast you're rowing; net worth tells you where the boat actually is. How to calculate it, track it, and use it to build wealth. "How to Pay Off Debt Faster: A Strategy Guide That Respects Both Math and Psychology" Avalanche, snowball, consolidation and balance transfers compared honestly — which debt payoff strategy fits your numbers and your temperament. "How to Price Freelance Work Without Underselling Yourself" Why new freelancers charge too little, how to calculate a rate that actually sustains you, and when to move from hourly to value-based pricing. "Renting vs Buying a Home: The Honest Math" Beyond 'rent is throwing money away' — the real costs on both sides, the break-even horizon, and how to decide based on your actual situation. "Roth vs Traditional: Which Retirement Account Wins?" A clear framework for choosing between Roth and Traditional retirement accounts — the tax logic, the break-even rule, and why many people use both.

Why FinCalc Lab?

Private by design

Every calculation happens in your browser. Your salary, debts and savings never leave your device.

The math, explained

Each tool shows the formula it uses and walks through what the result actually means for you.

No paywalls, ever

Free to use, no accounts, no "premium" upsell. Supported by unobtrusive advertising.

FinCalc Lab

Free, fast and honest financial calculators. All tools run entirely in your browser — we never see or store your numbers.

About Contact Privacy Policy Terms of Use

© 2026 FinCalc Lab. All calculators and articles are for educational purposes only and do not constitute financial advice. Consult a qualified professional before making financial decisions.
